category_title: First-grade students who struggle with independent writing and book selection
minimal_context: The student is at a workstation during daily reading class time and is tasked with writing a text-to-text connection in their journal.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please open your journals at your workstation and write a text-to-text connection to your book.

STUDENT: (Stares at the blank journal page) I don't know how to write my connection.

TUTOR: What do you think you should write?

STUDENT: I can't spell the words.

TUTOR: Just keep trying your best to write it down.

STUDENT: (Closes the journal and stops working) I don't want to write independently. I want to go listen to the audio books with a partner.

options.A: Break the goal into sub-targets: first week, write independently for 10 minutes; second week, write independently for 90 minutes.
options.B: Deliver 12 hour-long sessions, scheduled twice-weekly, of a mindfulness intervention program during daily reading class.
options.C: Conduct careful pre-intervention functional assessments to identify relationships between the environment and behavior.
options.D: Model the reading strategy being taught and share affective responses to the texts.
